food_name: Apam Balik (Thick Malaysian Pancake with Peanut, Sugar and Creamed Corn Filling)
food_subgroup: Leavened wheat flour griddle pancake with peanut-sugar-corn filling; thick (tebal) variety

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn URI exists for Apam Balik. Closest parent would be a generic pancake or griddle cake concept. URI withheld to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pancakes and similar griddle-cooked cereal products — used as closest-parent match for Apam Balik (Malay-Chinese leavened pancake with peanut-sugar filling)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Apam Balik. A0GBT (pancakes/griddle cakes) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for Apam Balik. Code withheld to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard hawker serving of Apam Balik is one half of a full round pancake, weighing approximately 100g, folded into a semicircle and cut into portions.

nutrient_source.title: Apam Balik — Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Apam Balik (thick variety). HPB Foodo is the primary approved source for Singapore hawker foods. Values are per 100g edible portion.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Wheat-Flour Pancakes and Analogous Leavened Griddle Cakes — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Apam Balik (thick) exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is estimated from the closest analogous foods: thick leavened wheat-flour pancakes and griddle cakes with refined sugar, which consistently return GI values of 66–74 in the Sydney database. The baking-powder leavening creates an open, porous crumb structure that accelerates starch gelatinisation and glucose release. The added refined sugar (20g per 100g) and creamed corn filling compound the glycaemic response. GL per 100g serving = GI × available carbohydrate / 100 = 70 × 45 / 100 ≈ 31.5. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~200mg per 100g); low saturated fat relative to portion size
hypertension.guidance: Apam Balik contains approximately 200mg sodium per 100g serving, which is moderate and not a primary sodium concern for hypertensive patients consuming a single hawker portion; however, patients on strict sodium restriction (<1500mg/day) should account for this contribution within their daily budget.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI ~70) and high glycemic load (~31.5 per 100g serving) driven by refined wheat flour, 20g added sugar per 100g, and creamed corn filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption to no more than half a pancake (~100g) per snack occasion and avoid daily intake, as the glycemic load of ~31.5 per serving represents approximately 30% of the recommended daily GL budget (~100) for T2DM adults; pairing with a protein source such as a boiled egg may partially attenuate the postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate load, added sugar (20g/100g), and trans fat (0.1g/100g) from margarine; saturated fat 3g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high refined-carbohydrate and added-sugar content of Apam Balik contributes to postprandial triglyceride elevation, a recognised cardiovascular risk factor; patients with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should treat this as an occasional food and limit portion to one half-pancake, noting the small but non-zero trans fat contribution from margarine used in some hawker preparations.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Apam Balik (also known as Ban Jian Kueh, Man Jian Gao, or Min Jian Kueh) is a thick, fluffy leavened wheat-flour pancake that is a quintessential street food of Singapore and Malaysia. The batter is made from wheat flour, eggs, sugar, baking soda or baking powder, and water or coconut milk, then poured onto a large round cast-iron griddle and cooked until the base is golden and the interior develops a soft, honeycomb-like crumb. While still hot, the pancake is filled with a generous layer of crushed roasted peanuts, granulated white sugar, creamed corn, and sometimes desiccated coconut, then folded in half to form a characteristic semicircle. The thick (tebal) version is soft and bready; the thin (nipis) version from Ipoh is crispy and fragile. A standard hawker serving is one half-pancake weighing approximately 100g. Per 100g: approximately 300 kcal, 6g protein, 10g fat (3g saturated), 47g carbohydrate, 20g sugar, 2g dietary fibre, 200mg sodium. Glycemic index is approximately 70 (HIGH) with a glycemic load of ~31.5 per serving — among the highest GL values of any Singapore hawker snack. Clinically, this food is high-concern for Type 2 Diabetes patients due to its compound glycaemic load from refined flour, added sugar, and corn. It is moderate-concern for cardiovascular disease due to added sugar and trace trans fat. Sodium is moderate and not a primary hypertension concern at this portion size. The food is vegetarian and halal. Common names: Apam Balik (Malay), Ban Jian Kueh (Hokkien/Singaporean Chinese), Martabak Manis (Indonesian).

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved source for Singapore hawker foods. GI estimated at 0.9 confidence from analogous leavened wheat-flour pancake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. All mandatory multilingual names present. Ontology anchored with closest-parent FoodEx2 code A0GBT with documented gap note.
notes: 1. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on thick leavened wheat-flour pancakes in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct Apam Balik GI measurement was found in the database at time of processing. 2. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat values are not reported by HPB Foodo for this entry; set to -1 per numeric hygiene rules. 3. Water content not reported by source; set to -1. 4. The FoodEx2 code A0GBT is used as a closest-parent match; no exact code for Apam Balik exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue. 5. FoodOn URI is null as no specific concept for Apam Balik was identified; gap note provided. 6. SNOMED CT code is null; no food concept identified. 7. Vitamin K (1.2 mcg/100g) and copper (0.1 mg/100g) and manganese (0.5 mg/100g) values are from enriched_json source with confidence 0.9; not directly reported by HPB Foodo. 8. All core macros are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for a leavened wheat-flour pancake with peanut-sugar filling.
